8-hour sleep, ending at 6:30am the next morning. Layla is an early riser and likely well-rested after a steady, grounded Friday. Sleep beat spans overnight as required.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather—77 degrees, breezy, the kind of Friday that makes you want to linger over coffee. Taught my usual classes at Lincoln, nothing earth-shattering, but I love that moment when a student's face clicks and they finally get it. Walked over to Capitol Hill for lunch at Molly Moon's, grabbed salted caramel, and just sat there with my film camera watching the world. The energy was already building—I could feel Pride creeping in, people getting ready, the streets starting to hum. Met Maya for early dinner at Serious Pie before the crowds took over. We caught the tail end of that warm, golden light walking back home through the quieter blocks. Spent the evening journaling, listening to music, prepping tomorrow's brewing setup. There's something grounding about that—the ritual of it. Heading to bed feeling like the city's about to throw a party and I'm tucked away in my corner, content.
thought_process: I chose to lean into the gentleness of the day—the weather was my anchor, so I let that shape my mood. Molly Moon's felt like a natural pause, a moment to step out of the teaching grind and just observe. I was aware of Pride building around me, but I didn't feel compelled to dive into the crowds; instead, I moved deliberately toward quieter moments and familiar spaces. Meeting Maya early was strategic—genuine connection before the evening exploded into festival chaos. By the time I got home, I was ready to slow down and be introspective: journaling, brewing prep, music. The day had a nice rhythm to it: structure, a small creative break, connection, then retreat into personal rituals.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
